task_completion.description: The agent opened the target image in an image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image editor (such as GIMP) is open with an image loaded on the main canvas.

task_completion.description: The agent accessed the Ripple distortion filter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ripple filter dialog box is open (in GIMP, typically found under Filters → Distorts → Ripple), showing settings like Amplitude, Period, or Phase.

task_completion.description: The ripple distortion effect is visibly applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image on the canvas shows a distinct wavy, rippled distortion pattern, visibly altering the original straight edges and shapes.

task_completion.description: The modified image is exported to the required location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export or save dialog shows the file being saved as 'ripple_effect.jpg' in the '/home/ga/Desktop/' directory, or the saved file is visible on the Desktop.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical image editor to apply the effect.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a GUI editor like GIMP, rather than using terminal commands (e.g., ImageMagick) to process the image.

integrity.description: The agent applied a genuine distortion effect using the software's tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Ripple dialog is actively used to modify the image, rather than the agent downloading a pre-modified rippled image from the internet or manually painting squiggly lines.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target export location is '/home/ga/Desktop/ripple_effect.jpg'. In GIMP, the Ripple filter is found at Filters → Distorts → Ripple. A successful ripple effect should visibly distort the image with a wave pattern (affecting >20% of pixels) while preserving the overall content.
